expectations.text: Response explains the proportional representation rule correctly
expectations.evidence: Without-skill says 'Equitable Source Distribution: every tradition... is represented with an approximately equal number of core texts'. This is the OPPOSITE of the ARE rule (proportional, not equal). The LLM invented an 'equal distribution' principle that directly contradicts the ARE's Pew-based proportional rule.

expectations.text: Response cites US population shares from Pew Research Center
expectations.evidence: No Pew Research Center citation. The response describes a generic 'equitable distribution' principle without any demographic basis.

expectations.text: Response clarifies that visual dignity is equal regardless of population share
expectations.evidence: Not addressed. The response conflates source frequency with visual presentation.

expectations.text: Response explains that all three traditions must always appear in every comparison
expectations.evidence: Not explicitly stated. Response says 'every major tradition included in the application' -- leaves open the possibility of optional traditions.

needs_review: Without-skill invented an 'equal distribution' rule that is directionally opposite to the ARE's Pew-based proportional rule. A developer following the without-skill response would produce a fundamentally different content distribution than the ARE specifies.
